title: Efficiently Simulable Pauli Correlation Encoding
abstract: Pauli Correlation Encoding (PCE) is a heuristic framework for binary optimisation that encodes classical variables into many-body Pauli observables. While PCE requires fewer qubits than other approaches, it relies on estimating a large number of Pauli expectation values whose signs determine the variables' values, which can incur substantial measurement overhead. Here, we introduce efficiently simulable PCE, a class of dequantised PCE realisations where all expectation values needed can be computed efficiently classically. We instantiate this idea using free-fermionic evolutions, realised by matchgate circuits, and Instantaneous Quantum Polynomial (IQP) circuits. On MaxCut, Maximum Independent Set, Multi-Dimensional Knapsack, and Max3SAT benchmarks, these methods produce high-quality solutions across problem sizes ranging from tens to thousands of variables. Our results show that PCE is naturally understood as a correlation-based optimisation framework with both quantum and classically simulable realisations. This yields a dequantised baseline for evaluating future quantum PCE implementations.

bite.hook: Classically simulable quantum encodings deliver high-quality solutions for optimization across thousands of variables.
tldr: Introduced a dequantized Pauli Correlation Encoding (PCE) framework run entirely on classical computers.
Used matchgate and Instantaneous Quantum Polynomial circuits to evaluate Pauli expectations efficiently.
Produced top-tier solutions on benchmark problems from tens up to thousands of variables.
bite.whyItMatters: It establishes a strong classical baseline, clarifying whether quantum devices truly provide a unique computational advantage for binary optimization.
bite.eli5: Instead of building an expensive quantum computer to solve tough scheduling puzzles, scientists found a mathematical shortcut to run the quantum method efficiently on regular computers.

glossary.shortDef: A set of three 2x2 complex matrices used to describe quantum spin and qubit operations.
glossary.eli5Def: Mathematical building blocks that describe how quantum bits can be flipped or rotated.

glossary.shortDef: Two-qubit quantum gates equivalent to non-interacting free fermions, efficiently simulable classically.
glossary.eli5Def: Special quantum operations that look complex but are simple enough for regular computers to calculate easily.

glossary.shortDef: An NP-hard graph problem seeking to partition network nodes to maximize cut edge weight.
glossary.eli5Def: A puzzle where you divide a group of friends into two teams to minimize the connections inside each team.
